resolution: list-populated's 8 rows appear in the same order (complete -> incomplete -> git-only -> key-unused -> key-used-ssh-only -> key-used-both -> key-missing -> fragment-path-missing) in both media (FIELDS.md); detail-ssh-first's SSH-section-then-Git-section order matches; delete-choice's Git-identity-only-then-everything order matches; confirm-destructive's warning-then-target-list-then-default-focus order matches. Verified via html/*.png and tui/*.png side-by-side review.

Labels and helper copy (each row's per-state note, list-empty's first-run copy, detail-ssh-first's 'No Git identity configured for this alias' note, action-menu's per-action descriptions, clone-name-prompt's distinct-name constraint, delete-choice's two option descriptions, confirm-destructive's warning/default-focus copy, backup-notice's undo-story line) match verbatim: the /mui route files read from src/data/recipeFixtures.ts's identityManagerRows/identityManagerDeleteChoices exports, and internal/dummytui/surface_identitymanager.go mirrors the same literal strings as Go im* constants/imRows. Spot-checked list-populated, detail-ssh-first, delete-choice, confirm-destructive, backup-notice html/tui screenshot pairs.

The 8-entry MGR-02 state taxonomy (complete/incomplete/git-only/key-unused/key-used-ssh-only/key-used-both/key-missing/fragment-path-missing) and the 2-entry delete-choice option set (Git identity only / everything) appear in the same order with the same per-option copy in both media (list-populated.png, delete-choice.png html/tui pairs).

The 8 MGR-02 labels are byte-identical to internal/identity/state.go's locked State constants (complete/incomplete/git-only/key-unused/key-used-ssh-only/key-used-both/key-missing/fragment-path-missing — grep-verified across recipeFixtures.ts, the route files, and surface_identitymanager.go). delete-choice defaults to the safer 'Git identity only' option (never the irreversible 'everything' path) per MGR-06/§5, consistent in both media. The 'personal' identity (SSH Host personal.github.com, IdentitiesOnly yes, ~/.gitconfig.d/personal fragment) reuses the same recipe-accurate literals create-flow/git-screen already established.

All 8 named states from UX-DIRECTION.md §4(3) exist as distinct, navigable screens in both media (manifest.json screen list == surface_identitymanager.go ScreenDef IDs == route file titles), reachable via the SAME transitions in both: list-populated -> {action-menu (a), detail-ssh-first (v), list-empty (e)}; detail-ssh-first -> action-menu (a); action-menu -> {clone-name-prompt (c), delete-choice (d)}; clone-name-prompt -> backup-notice (w); delete-choice -> confirm-destructive (x); confirm-destructive -> backup-notice (y). Verified by TestIdentityManager_KeysFormAConnectedPathReachingEveryScreen (Go), manual html/tui screenshot review, and the surface-scoped dummy-nav e2e (TestDummyNavReachesAllScreens/identity-manager) reaching every screen including the 5 placeOverlay-composited modal screens through the running binary.

The mutation ceremony's beats 1+2+3 (§5) appear on the same steps in both media: delete-choice is the read-only preview of the choice itself, confirm-destructive is the explicit confirm step (default-focused 'No, cancel', irreversible path requires a typed confirm), and backup-notice shows both timestamped backup paths before any implied result. Managed-block containment isn't directly rendered on this surface (identity-manager's delete flow doesn't show a config-text preview the way create-flow/git-screen's confirm-write does — MGR-06's own §4(3) named-state list has no separate 'preview the exact text' state; the target files ARE named explicitly on confirm-destructive: SSH Host block, Git configuration path, key file path). Verified via delete-choice.png, confirm-destructive.png, backup-notice.png pairs.

Every intra-flow key (a/v/e/c/d/w/x/y) is shown in the keybar/footer of the screen where it applies in BOTH media (Keybar.tsx entries vs renderShellKeybar output); the identity-manager keys allocate a=action-menu/c=clone-name-prompt/d=delete-choice from the UX-DIRECTION §2 key-allocation table (never n/g, create-flow's/git-screen's own LaunchKeys — TestIdentityManager_IntraSurfaceKeysNeverReuseLaunchKeysNOrG); reserved keys (Esc/q/?//) are consistent across every screenshot pair reviewed. See CRITIQUE.md finding #2 for the one accepted label-WORDING difference (TUI shows the raw target screen ID, HTML shows a semantic action phrase — the key itself and its destination match).

identity-manager's §4(3) highest-risk affordance (MGR-06): delete-choice visually distinguishes 'Delete Git identity only' with a 2px green border + '✓ default' marker in both media (delete-choice.png html/tui pair), while 'Delete everything (SSH + Git + key)' carries a 1px red/error border and is explicitly described as 'never default-focused'. confirm-destructive.png (both media) shows the irreversible path's strongest confirm: a red '✗ This cannot be undone' warning, the exact 3 items removed named explicitly, and 'Default-focused: No, cancel. Type the identity name to confirm.' Machine-checkable: TestIdentityManager_DeleteChoiceSafeDefaultNeverDefaultsToEverything (Go) asserts the safer option is marked '✓ default' and the irreversible option never is.

Every one of the 8 MGR-02 state labels on list-populated/detail-ssh-first is rendered as glyph (✓/!/✗) PLUS the state's own WORD inside a bordered Chip (HTML) or inline text (TUI) — never color alone. Verified by manually reviewing list-populated.png with color semantics removed mentally: each row remains fully disambiguated by the label text alone ('complete', 'incomplete', 'git-only', 'key-unused', 'key-used-ssh-only', 'key-used-both', 'key-missing', 'fragment-path-missing'). Machine-checkable: TestIdentityManager_EightLabelTaxonomyAnchors (Go) asserts all 8 label WORDS appear in the rendered TUI output; the HTML route reads the same words from recipeFixtures.ts's identityManagerRows.

detail-ssh-first (MGR-03/MGR-07) deliberately targets the SSH-only 'work' identity: SSH details (Host, IdentityFile, IdentitiesOnly) are shown in section '1' (first), and the Git section explicitly states 'No Git identity configured for this alias — this identity is SSH-only. gitid never renders fabricated Git attributes here (MGR-03/MGR-07)' — proving the rule with a real absence rather than a hypothetical caveat. Identical wording/order in both media (detail-ssh-first.png html/tui pair).
